auth.signInSubtitle: Enter your credentials to access your account

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.allStoresHint: This is the cross-store task list. These tasks have a general web browser for public pages (search, tracking/logistics) plus email and messaging — but not a specific store's backend. To open a store's seller center, click the store on the left first, then create a task.
tasks.retryConfirm: This clears the task’s history and restarts it from scratch. Are you sure?
tasks.continueMessage: Continue the previous task — resume now, reuse the progress and data already on disk, and do not restart from scratch.
tasks.error_agent_loop: The AI agent got stuck in a repeated loop and was automatically stopped. Please retry the task.
tasks.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this task?
tasks.deleteConfirmCascade: This task has {{count}} sub-task(s). Deleting it will also remove the entire subtree and all on-disk files. Continue?
tasks.createFirstTask: Create your first task to get started
tasks.selectTaskHint: Select a task or click "+ New Task" to create one
tasks.selectStoreHint: Select a store to get started
tasks.autoRunHint: Task will auto-plan and execute. Use Stop to interrupt.
tasks.confirmRunHint: Task will pause after planning for your confirmation before executing.
tasks.chatPlaceholder: Type a message to the AI...
tasks.noStoreWarning: This task has no store assigned. It can use the general web browser for public pages, but not a specific store's seller center.
tasks.ziniaoWslNotRunning:
Ziniao is not running. WSL cannot launch Ziniao automatically.
Please download and run the launcher script on Windows:
  1. Download the .bat file below
  2. Double-click ziniao_webdriver.bat on Windows
  3. Wait for "ready" message, then retry
tasks.ziniaoWslWrongPort: Ziniao is running on Windows but not listening on the expected port. Please close Ziniao and re-launch it using the ziniao_webdriver.bat script.
tasks.ziniaoWslCannotLaunch:
WSL cannot launch Ziniao automatically.
Please download and run the launcher script on Windows:
  1. Download the .bat file below
  2. Double-click ziniao_webdriver.bat on Windows
  3. Wait for "ready" message, then retry
tasks.error_rate_limit: API rate limit reached. Please wait a moment and retry.
tasks.error_auth_failed: API authentication failed. Check your API key in Settings.
tasks.error_billing_error: Account billing issue. Check your Anthropic account.
tasks.error_overloaded: API is overloaded. Please try again later.
tasks.error_server_error: API server error. Please try again later.
tasks.error_invalid_request: Invalid API request. Check your configuration.
tasks.error_max_tokens: Response was too long. Try a simpler task.
tasks.error_ziniao_not_running: Ziniao is not running. Please download and run ziniao_webdriver.bat on Windows, then retry.
tasks.error_ziniao_wrong_port: Ziniao is running but on wrong port. Re-launch with ziniao_webdriver.bat.
tasks.error_ziniao_cannot_launch: Cannot launch Ziniao. Download and run ziniao_webdriver.bat on Windows.
tasks.error_server_restart: Server restarted while task was running.
tasks.error_design_failed: Design phase did not produce a plan.
tasks.modePlanDesc: Draft a plan, run after approval
tasks.modeTooltipForced: This task type always shows a plan for review before running.
tasks.planReadyConfirm: Plan is ready. Review above, then approve or edit.
tasks.planRevising: Revising plan based on your feedback...
tasks.planFeedbackHint: Send feedback to revise the plan, or confirm to execute

events.title: Events
events.createFirstEvent: Create your first event to start tracking
events.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this event?

workspace.title: Knowledge
workspace.deleteFileConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this file?
workspace.deleteFolderConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this folder and all its contents?
workspace.emptyState: Select a file from the tree to view and edit
workspace.knowledgeHint: Knowledge files are shared with AI agents
workspace.projectKnowledgeHint: Built-in knowledge & docs. Automatically updated from the cloud.
workspace.localKnowledgeHint: Agent-generated knowledge. Rules and tips shared across all stores.
workspace.projectKnowledgeReadOnly: Built-in project knowledge (read-only). Automatically updated from the cloud.
workspace.projectKnowledgeEditable: Built-in project knowledge (read-only). Automatically updated from the cloud.
workspace.localKnowledgeEditHint: This is your local knowledge — feel free to edit. It won't be overwritten.
workspace.noHistory: No git history for this file
workspace.builtinSkillHint: Built-in skill (read-only). Automatically updated from the cloud.
workspace.modeAISub: Tell AI, auto-writes to knowledge files
workspace.assistantWelcome: Tell me about your stores, processes, or procedures — I'll save it so agents can use it when running tasks.
workspace.assistantExample1: Our US store ships with FedEx, DHL for international
workspace.assistantExample2: Create a skill for checking inactive listings
workspace.assistantExample3: Store Alpha's seller account email is ops@example.com
workspace.assistantPlaceholder: Tell the AI something about your stores...
workspace.skillsHint: Reusable procedures & scripts. Agents load relevant skills automatically.
workspace.noSkills: No skills yet — agents create them automatically
workspace.storeFilesHint: Per-store knowledge. Agents see only the relevant store's files when running a task.
workspace.truncatedRows: Showing first {{shown}} of {{total}} rows — open the file for the rest.
workspace.storeDataHint: Per-store task outputs (reports, exports, captures) under store-data/. Same tree the agent reads/writes; not knowledge.
workspace.noStoresInWorkspace: No stores yet — create a store in Tasks view
workspace.hintSkillMd: Skill instructions — name, tools, step-by-step procedures
workspace.importedSkillHint: Imported skill — local edits may diverge from the original source.
workspace.uninstallSkillConfirm: Uninstall skill "{{name}}"? This will delete all skill files.
workspace.noCustomSkills: No custom skills yet — create one or ask the assistant
workspace.noImportedSkills: No imported skills yet — ask the assistant to install from a URL

settings.title: Settings
settings.deleteUserConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
settings.ziniaoConnectError: Ziniao is not running or not in WebDriver mode.
settings.ziniaoLaunchHint: Steps: 1) Download the .bat file below  2) Double-click it on Windows  3) Wait until it shows "ready"  4) Click Refresh
settings.ziniaoNoProfiles: No browser profiles found. Please check your Ziniao account.
settings.ziniaoRunningNormalMode: Ziniao is running but not in WebDriver mode.
settings.ziniaoNormalModeHint: Please close Ziniao and click Refresh.
settings.ziniaoStillRunning: Ziniao is still running. It may not have fully exited.
settings.ziniaoNotInstalledHint: Download and install from the official website.
settings.ziniaoNoPermission: WebDriver is not enabled for this account.
settings.ziniaoNoPermissionHint: The BOSS account must enable WebDriver in the developer console.
settings.ziniaoNewTerminalLogin: Ziniao detected a login from a new device. Please open Ziniao manually to complete login verification, then come back and retry.
settings.ziniaoRestartFailed: Failed to restart. Please close Ziniao manually and try again.
settings.ziniaoConnectErrorMac: Cannot connect to Ziniao. Please check if Ziniao is installed.
settings.ziniaoCredentialsError: The stored password can't be decrypted on this machine (the secret used to encrypt it doesn't match this install). Click Edit to re-enter the password.
settings.enterStoreNameFirst: Please enter a store name first
settings.deleteAccountConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this Ziniao account?
settings.deleteStoreConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this store?
settings.adminRequired: Admin access required to manage settings.
settings.requireLoginDesc: When enabled, users must log in to access the app.
settings.confirmDeleteUser: Delete user "{{username}}"? This cannot be undone.
settings.skillsAutoSyncDesc: When on (default), the agent checks GitHub once every 24h and pulls new or updated built-in skills before each task. Turn off to pin the locally-bundled version — the periodic check is skipped, but you can still trigger a one-off sync from the Skills page.
settings.browserHeadlessDesc: When on (default), browsers run in the background. Turn off to make every browser the agent drives — including the auxiliary Chrome session for non-seller-center URLs — visible on your screen.
settings.browserHeadlessWarn: Switching this restarts auxiliary browser sessions. Tasks currently using one may be interrupted. Continue?
settings.channelConfigHint: Configure message channels for event extraction.
settings.eventSyncConfigHint: Configure where extracted events are synced to.
settings.dida365GuideStep2: Register an app on the developer portal
settings.dida365GuideStep2Hint: Click the link below to open the developer portal. Sign in, then click "New App". Enter any Name (e.g. vibe-seller), leave App Service URL and Description empty, then click "Add".
settings.dida365GuideStep3Hint: Go back to the app list, click "Edit" next to the app you just created. Find the "OAuth Redirect URL" field, paste the URL below, then click "Save":
settings.dida365GuideStep4: On the same edit page, copy the Client ID and Client Secret, then paste them here
settings.dida365ClientIdPlaceholder: Paste Client ID from developer portal
settings.dida365ClientSecretPlaceholder: Paste Client Secret from developer portal
settings.dida365GuideStep5: Make sure you've saved everything in the developer portal, then click below to start OAuth authorization
settings.dida365Installing: Installing TickTick MCP tools (first time only)...
settings.dida365SetupFailed: Failed to install TickTick MCP: {{error}}. Make sure git and uv are installed.
settings.dida365Connecting: Waiting for authorization — complete the login in the popup window...
settings.dida365DisconnectConfirm: Disconnect TickTick integration? AI agent will lose access to TickTick tools.
settings.dida365ProjectHint: Event sync will create tasks in this project. Leave empty to let the AI access all projects.
settings.dida365AgentInfo: AI agent can read and write your TickTick/Dida365 projects and tasks.
settings.dida365DisconnectHint: Disconnecting will remove AI access to TickTick/Dida365
settings.dida365PortNote: Note: If you change the server port, you need to update this URL in the developer portal too.
settings.dida365BareHostWarning: Dida365/TickTick developer portal only accepts localhost or a full domain (e.g. host.example.com). Bare hostnames like "{{hostname}}" will be rejected.
settings.dida365BareHostHint: Fix: open a browser on the server machine itself, visit http://localhost:{{port}} to complete OAuth there, then come back to this remote page — it will work normally. Or access this page via a full domain (e.g. Tailscale *.ts.net).
settings.dida365PopupHint: The authorization page opens in a popup. If your browser blocks it, choose "Always allow pop-ups" and retry.
settings.dida365PopupBlocked: Pop-up was blocked by the browser
settings.dida365PopupBlockedHint: Click the pop-up blocked icon in the address bar, select "Always allow pop-ups from this site", then click below to retry.
settings.maxConcurrencyDesc: Maximum number of agents running simultaneously
settings.taskRetentionDesc: Daily job removes completed/failed tasks older than this. Set to 0 to disable.
settings.telemetryDesc: Send anonymous install + task counts to help improve the product. No business content (titles, products, screenshots, chat) is ever sent. See docs/telemetry.md.
settings.defaultScheduleTimezoneDesc: Used when creating new schedules without an explicit timezone
settings.proxyHint: Route browser traffic through a proxy

gws.title: Google Workspace
gws.description: Expose Sheets, Drive, Gmail, Docs, and Calendar to agents via the gws CLI. Installs 19 sub-skills under a single umbrella.
gws.installing: Installing skills — this can take 20–30s while gws generates docs for all APIs…
gws.installedHint: Agents can invoke the gws umbrella skill.
gws.notInstalledHint: Toggle on to install the skill bundle into your workspace.

wecom_bot.title: WeChat Work Bot
wecom_bot.description: Send messages to WeChat Work groups via bot webhooks. Configure one or more bots — each stores a name and webhook URL.
wecom_bot.name: Name
wecom_bot.webhookHint: Get the webhook URL from your WeChat Work group bot settings.
wecom_bot.maskedHint: Webhook URL is hidden to protect its key. Click Edit to view or change it.

stores.noStores: No stores yet. Create a store from the sidebar in Tasks view.
stores.deleteWarning: This will permanently delete store "{{name}}". Tasks and events will be preserved but unlinked from this store.
stores.deleteFilesHint: Removes store knowledge (~/.vibe-seller/stores/{slug}/) and browser profile (~/.vibe-seller/browser_profiles/{slug}/).
stores.activeTasksError: Cannot perform this action while the store has active tasks
stores.nameExistsError: A store with this name already exists

chat.profileMismatch: Task was run by {{current}}. Different providers may be incompatible — results are not guaranteed if switched

profiles.modalHint: Profiles define environment variables injected before launching Claude Code CLI. Use them to switch AI providers, set API keys, or customize behavior.
profiles.description: Create custom profiles with different environment variables for different AI providers
profiles.noProfiles: No custom profiles yet. Create one to get started.
profiles.confirmDelete: Delete profile "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
profiles.loadGlobalMcpHint: Include MCP servers from global Claude settings. Disable to reduce tool count and speed up third-party APIs.

externalConfigOverride.title: External config conflicts with this profile
externalConfigOverride.intro: Profile "{{profileId}}" can't be used: {{settingsPath}} has env entries that take precedence and silently route the agent to another endpoint:
externalConfigOverride.optionDefault: Switch your profile to "Claude" (default) and let the external tool (e.g. cc-switch) own provider routing.
externalConfigOverride.optionClear: Quit the tool that wrote it (cc-switch / similar), then run this once to clear the conflicting env block:

schedules.title: Scheduled Tasks

plan.heading: Plan
plan.waitingForApproval: Planning task is awaiting your approval.

explainer.ready: A plan is frozen on this schedule and will be reused for every fire.
explainer.planning: An agent is authoring the plan. Open the planning task to review and approve it.
explainer.stale: The schedule prompt changed — the previous plan is no longer used. Re-plan before the next fire.
explainer.failed: The planner failed. Re-plan to try again.
explainer.none: No plan has been authored yet. Start planning to create one.

finalize.heading: Finalize step (registered by the agent)
finalize.explainer: After all stores finish, one extra step runs to combine their results. No action needed.

schedules.newScheduleSubtitle: What should the AI do on a recurring basis?
schedules.deleteConfirm: Delete this schedule? Child tasks will be kept.
schedules.createFirstSchedule: Create a schedule to automate recurring tasks
schedules.fanoutModeDescription: Create one task per active store on each tick — good for per-store work.
schedules.singleModeDescription: Create exactly one task (no store binding) on each tick — good for shared work like IMAP sweeps.
schedules.modeLocked: Mode and store are fixed at creation.
schedules.defaultModeHelp: Default mode pre-selected when creating a new 'All Stores' schedule.

updateCheck.title: Update available
updateCheck.versionLine: v{{current}} is out of date — v{{latest}} is now available.
updateCheck.windowsInstructions: Download the latest installer and run it — it upgrades in place:
